description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with a secondary, lower gable extension on one side, giving it an asymmetrical profile. The exterior is clad in horizontal wooden siding with a brick foundation visible at the base. Large rectangular windows are distributed across both stories, with a prominent set of three windows on the ground floor of the side extension. The main facade includes a central entry door flanked by windows, and the upper story has two vertically aligned windows. The roof is finished in a muted green metal, and the overall design suggests a blend of modern and rustic architectural influences. The house appears spacious, with ample natural light provided by the numerous windows.
description: A two-story house with a steep green gable roof, wood siding, and large rectangular windows, including a side extension with multiple windows. The design blends modern and rustic elements with an asymmetrical layout.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gable, central entry door, and two stories of windows, with the side extension visible on the left.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the green metal gable roof and the roof extension over the side wing.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the steep gable, two vertically aligned windows, and the projecting side extension.
description_view_3: Rear/top view showing the back of the house, the three large windows of the side extension, and the roof's intersecting forms.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large rectangular windows and vertical alignment on main facade
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry door with minimal overhang, flanked by windows, no porch or decorative columns
